title: Character Counter
description: Count characters, words, and lines in real-time. Supports full-width / half-width distinction. Free online tool.
label: Input Text

howToUse.label: Enter text
howToUse.description: Type in the text area or click the Paste button to paste from clipboard.

howToUse.label: View real-time results
howToUse.description: Character count, word count, and line count update automatically as you type.

howToUse.label: Check character limits
howToUse.description: Use the progress bars to see how much of each platform's character limit you have used.

howToUse.label: Copy or clear
howToUse.description: Use Copy to save your text to the clipboard, or Clear to reset the input.

faq.question: Can I check character limits for Twitter/X, Instagram, or other platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool includes real-time progress bars for common platform limits (Twitter/X: 280, Instagram caption: 2,200, and more). The bar turns red when you exceed a limit, making it easy to trim your text before posting.

faq.question: What is the difference between characters and bytes?
faq.answer: A character is what you see — one letter, space, or emoji counts as 1. Bytes depend on the encoding: ASCII characters are 1 byte in UTF-8, Japanese characters are 3 bytes, and most emoji are 4 bytes. If a database or API enforces a byte limit rather than a character limit, check the Bytes (UTF-8) counter.

faq.question: How are full-width and half-width characters counted?
faq.answer: Full-width characters (Japanese kana/kanji, full-width alphanumerics) and half-width characters (ASCII, half-width katakana) are counted separately in their own fields. The main character count treats both as 1 character each.

faq.question: Is my text sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your text is never sent to any external server.
